en.extract:
The red-capped myzomela (Myzomela lafargei), also known as the scarlet-naped myzomela, is a species of bird in the family Meliphagidae.  It is found in the Solomon Islands archipelago (Buka, Bougainville, the Shortland Islands, Fauro, Choiseul and Santa Isabel).  Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests, subtropical or tropical mangrove forests, and subtropical or tropical moist montane forests.

The '''red-capped myzomela''' ('''''Myzomela lafargei'''''), also known as the '''scarlet-naped myzomela''', is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Meliphagidae]].  It is found in the [[Solomon Islands (archipelago)|Solomon Islands archipelago]] ([[Buka Island|Buka]], [[Bougainville Island|Bougainville]], the [[Shortland Islands]], [[Fauro Island|Fauro]], [[Choiseul Island|Choiseul]] and [[Santa Isabel Island|Santa Isabel]]).  Its natural [[habitat]]s are subtropical or tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s, subtropical or tropical mangrove forests, and subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]]s.
